content_hints.description: Trollskull Manor lists to the left like a drunk leaning on a lamppost. Three stories of cracked plaster and warped timber, its tap room still smells of pipe smoke and old ale despite standing vacant for a decade. The floorboards groan beneath your feet, and candles gutter without any draft.
content_hints.read_aloud: The manor lists to the left like a drunk leaning on a lamppost, three stories of cracked plaster and warped timber rising above a tap room thick with the ghost-smell of pipe smoke and old ale. Floorboards groan beneath your weight. Dust sheets drape the furniture like burial shrouds, and candles in their sconces gutter and flare without any draft you can feel. Somewhere upstairs, a door creaks open on its own.

npcs.name: Lif
npcs.dialogue: Glasses slide across the bar of their own accord. A rag polishes the taps in slow, deliberate circles. The word WELCOME appears, letter by letter, in the condensation on the window.
npcs.notes: Former barkeep, died in the taproom. Poltergeist activity -- hostile until the party renovates the manor, then becomes a loyal (if invisible) employee. Moves objects, cleans, and occasionally throws things at rude customers.

event_triggers: On entry: Lif slams a door shut behind the party, then slowly opens it again -- testing their nerve.
On renovation complete: Lif begins serving drinks to patrons, glasses floating through the air.

content_hints.description: Sawdust carpets the floor of this narrow workshop, and the air tastes of fresh-cut pine and linseed oil. Half-finished chairs hang from ceiling hooks like wooden bats. A wood elf works a hand-plane with meditative precision, shavings curling away in pale ribbons.
content_hints.read_aloud: Sawdust carpets the floor of this narrow workshop, muffling your footsteps. The air is sweet with fresh-cut pine and linseed oil. Half-finished chairs hang from ceiling hooks overhead, turning slowly, and pale wood shavings curl across the workbench where a figure draws a hand-plane in long, meditative strokes. The sound is rhythmic and calming -- the quiet rasp of blade on grain.

npcs.name: Talisolvanar 'Tally' Fellbranch
npcs.dialogue: Every joint tells a story. Dovetail means trust. Mortise-and-tenon means permanence. Nails? Nails mean you're in a hurry, and hurry is the enemy of craft.
npcs.notes: Wood elf carpenter. Patient, philosophical. Will repair furniture and sell custom pieces. Knows every building on the alley and notices structural changes -- useful for detecting break-ins or hidden passages.

content_hints.description: Heat rolls out the open door in visible waves, carrying the tang of hot iron and quenching oil. Inside, a fire genasi hammers a glowing blade while a water genasi tempers another in a stone trough. Steam hisses between them like a conversation neither needs to speak aloud.
content_hints.read_aloud: Heat rolls out the open door in visible waves, and the tang of hot iron hits you before you cross the threshold. Inside, sparks fountain from an anvil where a figure wreathed in faint flame hammers a glowing blade. Across the room, another figure plunges steel into a stone trough with a violent hiss of steam. The forge light paints everything in shades of orange and red, and the rhythmic ring of hammer on metal vibrates in your chest.

npcs.name: Avi
npcs.dialogue: Embric does the shaping, I do the finishing. He is fire, I am patience. Between us, we make things that last.
npcs.notes: Water genasi. Quiet, precise. Handles the business side. Married to Embric.

npcs.name: Embric
npcs.dialogue: You want sharp? I give you sharp. You want beautiful? That costs extra. You want both? Now we are talking.
npcs.notes: Fire genasi. Passionate, dramatic. Forges weapons and armor. Married to Avi.

content_hints.description: Bundles of dried herbs hang from every rafter, filling the shop with a green, medicinal perfume that clings to your clothes long after you leave. A wood elf tends rows of potted plants on windowsill shelves, murmuring to each one as she waters them. A fat orange tabby sleeps in a patch of sunlight on the counter.
content_hints.read_aloud: Bundles of dried herbs hang from every rafter, so thick they form a rustling canopy overhead. The air is green and medicinal, sweet with lavender and sharp with something astringent that clears your sinuses. Potted plants crowd the windowsills, their leaves pressing against the glass, and a fat orange tabby is curled on the counter in a patch of sunlight, one ear twitching as you enter.

npcs.name: Fala Lefaliir
npcs.dialogue: This one is for headaches. This one is for heartbreak. No, I am not joking -- grief sits in the body like a stone, and willowbark alone will not shift it.
npcs.notes: Non-binary wood elf herbalist. Gentle, perceptive. Sells potions and herbal remedies. Member of the Emerald Enclave. Knows local gossip through their customers' ailments.

content_hints.description: A brass placard reads TIGER'S EYE: PRIVATE INVESTIGATIONS in letters that have been polished so often they've worn thin. Inside, the office is immaculate -- not a single paper out of place, not a mote of dust on the mahogany desk. The proprietor watches you enter with eyes that miss nothing and give away less.
content_hints.read_aloud: A brass placard beside the door reads TIGER'S EYE: PRIVATE INVESTIGATIONS, its letters polished to a mirror sheen. Inside, the office is immaculate -- dark mahogany desk, leather chairs, a filing cabinet closed with a heavy lock. Not a single paper is out of place, not a mote of dust on any surface. The figure behind the desk watches you enter with a gaze that takes in everything and offers nothing back. The room smells of ink and expensive tobacco.

npcs.name: Vincent Trench
npcs.dialogue: Everyone on this alley has a secret. I know most of them. The interesting question is not what people hide -- it is why they think hiding it will help.
npcs.notes: Actually a disguised Rakshasa named Valetta. Operates a legitimate detective agency as deep cover. Genuinely helpful to neighbors -- the disguise has become comfortable. Will investigate leads for the party at a price. Detects as fiend if checked.

event_triggers: On quest accept: Vincent provides a dossier on the target with unsettling accuracy.

content_hints.description: The alley narrows here between leaning tenements, the cobblestones slick with something best not examined too closely. A gutter runs down the center carrying rainwater and the occasional rat. Beyond, the noise of the North Ward swells -- cart wheels, hawkers, the Watch patrol's bootfall.
content_hints.read_aloud: The alley narrows between leaning tenements until you could touch both walls with outstretched arms. The cobblestones are slick beneath your boots, and a gutter runs down the center carrying dark water and the occasional scurrying shape. Beyond the alley's mouth, the noise of the North Ward swells -- the rumble of cart wheels, the calls of hawkers, and the measured bootfall of a Watch patrol passing by.
event_triggers: On entry: A street urchin offers to carry a message anywhere in Waterdeep for 2 copper.
